January 7, 1988


Jane Sands
449 San Bruno Avenue
Brisbane, CA 94005

                                    Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                         Our File No. I-87-326

Dear Ms. Sands:

     We have received your letter questioning signatures on
nomination papers for a Brisbane City Council candidate.

     The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. 1/  The
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions,
disclosure of assets and income of public officials,
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on
public officials.

     The question you pose in your letter is not covered by the
Political Reform Act.  Therefore, we cannot advise you on this
matter.  It would be more appropriate for you to direct your
question to the Elections and Political Reform Division of the
Secretary of State's Office at (916) 322-0392.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    Diane M. Griffiths
                                    General Counsel

                                    [signature]
                                    By:  Jeanette Turvill
                                         Legal Assistant

JET:jaj

_______________________

1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 201
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

December 28, 1981

Dear Person,
    I wish to share these concerns with you:

1) A review of signatures on these enclosed nomination
papers reveal only 5 signatures without question.
I did not question signatures missing a Ms, Mrs. or Mr.
designation but have been told such designation
must be included for voting purposes. According
to the rules applicable for referendum signing, the
signatures I questioned would most likely be
thrown out.

I now doubt that the candidate is legal. Where do
I register my complaint? My city has only
1100 households, so can be considered small. Our
City Clerk the person responsible for verifying
signatures is both lacking in knowledge and co-operative
spirit. Please advise me at what to do about
this.

2) See enclosed sheet - the same clerk marked the X
and made the note she signed as city clerk.
The candidate did not follow instructions and provided
schedules she was not required to provide. I want
copies of what the candidate filed as a matter
of public record. The clerk refuses to provide

P3  1of2  showing errors in signatures.

1.  Missing  CA, and zip code

2. Missing zip code, d from Reed designation, and D. as
    initial from voter registration list.

3. Missing zip code and J. as initial from voter
    registration list.

4. Missing zip code

5. Missing zip code and not on my voter registration list.

6. Missing zip code and not on my voter registration list.

7. Name not same as on voter registration list, missing middle name.

8. Missing city, CA, and zip code on proper line. Name not
    same as on voter registration list, missing first name.

9. Hold  public office under another name.

10. Missing  designation for street. Missing initial for middle
     name as known on voter registration list.

11.

12. Missing CA, and zip code. Missing initial for middle
     name as shown on voter registration list.

13. Missing zip code

14. Not on my voter registration list.

15.

16.

17.

Pa 2 of 2 showing errors in signatures.

.18. Missing designation for street. Not on my list of registered voters.

.19. Missing designation for street. Missing middle initial.

.20.

.21 Missing designation for street and CA.

.22. Missing middle initial.

.23.

.24. Missing signature and designations for JR., road and CA. Also missing zip code.

.25. Missing middle initial.

.26. Missing middle initial.

.27. Missing middle initial.

.28. Missing middle initial, designation for CA and zip code.

.29. Missing middle initial and zip code.

.30. Missing middle initial, designation for CA and zip code.

OFFICIAL FILING FORM

[illegible signature]
By__________________ Deputy
JULY 27 19 87
The candidate herein files his signatures
at the time of issuance. See 22641.

Affidavit of Circulator
(Sections 44, 53, 6862, 22838, 22841, Elections Code)

I, ....SUSAN.....NIELSEN...................................................., circulated the foregoing
petition and saw all the signatures appended thereto and to my best information and belief, each signature is the genuine signature of the
person whose name it purports to be; my residence voting address is ...290....TRINITY....ROAD....

nominee for the office of BRISBANE...CITY...COUNCIL MEMBER...

[Illegible designation above.]

....AUGUST..................... 19...87.....

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

and domestic; that I will bear true faith and allegiance to the Constitution of the United States and the Constitution of the State of California;
that I take this obligation freely, without any mental reservation or purpose of evasion; and that I will well and faithfully discharge the duties
upon which I am to enter.

Name___Nissen

The Following Summary Must Be Completed By All Filers

SCHEDULE A—Investments

SCHEDULE D—Income (other than Gifts and Loans)

☒ Schedule completed and attached                    ☒ No reportable interests

SCHEDULE E—Loans (Received or Outstanding During the Period)

☒ Schedule completed and attached                    ☐ No reportable interests

SCHEDULE F—Gifts

☐ Schedule completed and attached                    ☒ No reportable interests

SCHEDULE G—Honoraria

☐ Schedule completed and attached                    ☒ No reportable interests

SCHEDULE H—Commission Income and Income and Loans to Business Entities

☒ Schedule completed and attached                    ☐ No reportable interests

NOTE: After the summary page has been completed, please discard those Schedules on which you have no reportable interests.

VERIFICATION

I have used all reasonable diligence in preparing this Statement. I have reviewed the Statement and to the best of my knowledge the information contained herein and in the attached schedules is true and complete.

I certify under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct.

Executed on___3____/____3____/____, 19_87_, at___Burbank, Cal_____
                                                    (place)

SIGNATURE___Susan M. Neilson_____

Filing this Statement may not relieve you of the obligation to file under the Conflict of Interest Code for any other agency. Consult your filing officer or the Fair Political Practices Commission.

All of the information required by this form is mandatory, is required by the provisions of the Political Reform Act, and will be available to any member of the public upon request. This information is to be used to reveal to public scrutiny certain financial interests of public officials in order to disclose potential conflicts of interests and to aid in the prevention of actual conflicts of interests.

NOTE: To expedite service, if you need copies of all Statements filed with you and forwarded the originals within five days of the filing deadline (or if filed late, within five days of receipt) to: The Fair Political Practices Commission, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division, P.O. Box 807, Sacramento, California 95804-0807, (916) 322-5662.

Page 2
721(10/86)

A6 ■ Thursday, October 15, 1987 Marin Independent Journal

Boxer backing Simon in primaries

By Bill O'Driscoll
Gannett News Service

WASHINGTON — Rep. Barbara Boxer says she will back Illinois Sen. Paul Simon for the 1988 Democratic presidential nomination now that Sen. Joe Biden of Delaware is out of the race.

"What he (Simon) brings is an incredible background of honesty and character," Boxer said in an interview Wednesday.

"He goes against every rule when it comes to a candidate having to be

a handsome 'Mr. Personality,'" she said. "If everyone could get over someone who wears bow ties and has 'hopoe' ears and then look at his depth, it will test the depth of the American electorate."

Boxer gets her first test of Simon support from her Marin County constituents when she co-hosts a fund-raising dinner on Sunday with John Burton, her predecessor in Congress.

Simon will attend the $50-a-plate get-together at the home of Mill Valley resident Burton Wise.

Boxer said she felt no bitterness

over Biden's departure from the race after his views made of quoting from other politicans' speeches and not giving them credit, and exaggerating his law school accomplishments.

The quote-lifting was a "non-issue," Boyer said, but padding his own academic record was "more damaging."

This candidate claims so I.Q. we think she padded. This stuff ends national careers but at the local level how do you deal with these lies?

embedding.qa_text: **California Fair Political Practices Commission** January 7, 1988 Jane Sands 449 San Bruno Avenue Brisbane, CA 94005 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-87-326 Dear Ms. Sands: We have received your letter questioning signatures on nomination papers for a Brisbane City Council candidate. The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. 1/ The Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, disclosure of assets and income of public officials, disqualification of public officials from decisions which could affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on public officials. The question you pose in your letter is not covered by the Political Reform Act. Therefore, we cannot advise you on this matter. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your question to the Elections and Political Reform Division of the Secretary of State's Office at (916) 322-0392. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel By: Jeanette Turvill Legal Assistant JET:jaj 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 December 28, 1987 Dear Person, I wish to share these concerns with you: 1) A review of signatures on these enclosed nomination papers reveals only 5 signatures without question. I did not question signatures missing a Ms., Mrs., or Mr. designation but have been told such designation must be included for voting purposes. According to the rules applicable for referendum signing, the signatures I questioned would most likely be thrown out. I now doubt that this candidate is legal. Where do I register my complaint? My city has only 1100 households, so can be considered small. Our City Clerk, the person responsible for verifying signatures, is both lacking in knowledge and co-operative spirit. Please advise me of what to do about this. 2) See enclosed sheet—the same clerk marked the X and made the note she signed as city clerk. The candidate did not follow instructions and provided schedules she was not required to provide. I want copies of what the candidate filed as a matter of public record. The clerk refuses to provide Pg 1 of 2 showing errors in signatures. 1. Missing CA. and zip code 2. Missing zip code, d from Road designation, and D. as initial from voter registration list. 3. Missing zip code and J. as initial from voter registration list. 4. Missing zip code 5. Missing zip code and not on my voter registration list. 6. Missing zip code and not on my voter registration list. 7. Name not same as on voter registration list, missing middle name. 8. Missing city, CA, and zip code on proper line. Name not same as on voter registration list, missing first name. 9. Holds public office under another name. 10. Missing designation for street. Missing initial for middle name as shown on voter
